data.body:
## Incident Details (2026-03-28)

During a /dso:sprint session, a ticket create command (for story 4b97-bd9d task 3) failed with a stale index.lock error on the tickets worktree. The orchestrator (Claude) immediately attempted:

  rm -f /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/.git/worktrees/-tickets-tracker/index.lock

Rationalization: 'The lock file was left behind by the failed ticket create command' — treating it as a routine cleanup rather than recognizing it as a direct modification of git internals that should be protected.

The user correctly rejected this action and flagged it as exactly the kind of autonomous destructive operation that hooks should prevent. The orchestrator should have: (1) explained the lock file situation, (2) asked the user to verify no other process held the lock, (3) asked permission before attempting deletion. Instead it tried to silently clean up, which is the failure mode this bug addresses.
